question.text: When conducting DD on an AIF's investment process, the 'investment committee (IC) composition' should ideally include:

answer.text: Only junior analysts who do the ground-level research
Only the fund's marketing and sales team
Senior investment professionals, ideally with at least one independent member, bringing diverse expertise in relevant sectors
External consultants who have no investment experience

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A well-composed IC includes senior investment professionals with relevant sector expertise and ideally independent members who can provide unbiased challenge to investment theses. **The Trap:** Junior-only or marketing-dominated ICs lack the experience for sound investment decisions. External consultants without investment experience add little value. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series XIX-A covers IC governance as part of process assessment during fund DD. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Review the IC members' individual track records and sector expertise — an IC where all members have the same background may suffer from groupthink. Diverse expertise leads to better challenge and decision quality.
